questions.q: In which field did C. R. Rao make his most celebrated contributions?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao was a statistician whose work forms cornerstones of modern statistical theory.

questions.q: Which famous lower bound on the variance of an estimator carries C. R. Rao's name?
questions.explanation: The Cramer-Rao bound (or inequality) sets a lower limit on the variance of any unbiased estimator.

questions.q: Which theorem for improving an estimator is named jointly for Rao and another statistician?
questions.explanation: The Rao-Blackwell theorem describes how to improve an estimator using a sufficient statistic.

questions.q: What does the initials 'C. R.' stand for in C. R. Rao's name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Calyampudi Radhakrishna Rao.

questions.q: C. R. Rao was associated for decades with which pioneering Indian statistical institution?
questions.explanation: Rao spent much of his career at the Indian Statistical Institute (ISI) in Kolkata.

questions.q: C. R. Rao was a citizen and native of which country?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao was born in India and is one of its most celebrated scientists.

questions.q: The Cramer-Rao bound is most directly concerned with which property of an estimator?
options: The lower limit of its variance
questions.explanation: The Cramer-Rao bound gives a theoretical lower bound on the variance of an unbiased estimator.

questions.q: Which mathematical quantity is central to the Cramer-Rao bound?
questions.explanation: The Cramer-Rao bound expresses the variance limit as the inverse of the Fisher information.

questions.q: C. R. Rao is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Rao was a mathematician and statistician renowned for foundational results in estimation theory.

questions.q: The Rao-Blackwell theorem improves an estimator by conditioning on what kind of statistic?
questions.explanation: Rao-Blackwellization conditions an estimator on a sufficient statistic to reduce its variance.

questions.q: In which decade did C. R. Rao publish the seminal results that led to the Cramer-Rao bound and Rao-Blackwell theorem?
questions.explanation: Rao's foundational papers appeared in the mid-1940s, around 1945.

questions.q: C. R. Rao earned his PhD at the University of Cambridge under which renowned statistician?
questions.explanation: Rao completed his doctorate at Cambridge with R. A. Fisher as his adviser.

questions.q: Which test, used to compare nested statistical models, is named after Rao?
questions.explanation: Rao's score test (the Lagrange multiplier test) is a classic large-sample hypothesis test he introduced.

questions.q: Who is the 'Cramer' in the Cramer-Rao bound, discovered independently around the same time?
questions.explanation: The Swedish statistician Harald Cramer derived the same inequality independently of Rao.

questions.q: Which prestigious U.S. honor did C. R. Rao receive in 2002 for his statistical achievements?
options: The U.S. National Medal of Science
questions.explanation: In 2002 Rao was awarded the U.S. National Medal of Science, the country's highest scientific honor.

questions.q: In 2023, C. R. Rao received which award often called the 'Nobel Prize of statistics'?
questions.explanation: Rao was awarded the 2023 International Prize in Statistics, widely regarded as statistics' top honor.

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Indian Statistical Institute where Rao spent much of his career?
questions.explanation: P. C. Mahalanobis founded the Indian Statistical Institute, where Rao worked and rose to prominence.

questions.q: The Cramer-Rao bound is satisfied with equality by which class of estimators when they exist?
questions.explanation: An estimator that attains the Cramer-Rao bound is called efficient, achieving the minimum possible variance.

questions.q: Rao made early contributions to which area concerning the geometry of statistical models?
questions.explanation: Rao's use of the Fisher information as a Riemannian metric helped launch information geometry.

questions.q: Which distance measure between probability distributions bears Rao's name?
questions.explanation: The Rao distance is a geodesic distance on a statistical manifold derived from the Fisher information metric.

questions.q: Beyond estimation theory, Rao contributed influential methods to which applied field?
questions.explanation: Rao made major contributions to multivariate statistical analysis and its applications in biometry.

questions.q: C. R. Rao's influential textbook is commonly titled 'Linear Statistical Inference and Its ___'.
questions.explanation: Rao authored the classic text 'Linear Statistical Inference and Its Applications'.

questions.q: Who is the 'Blackwell' associated with the Rao-Blackwell theorem?
questions.explanation: American statistician David Blackwell independently derived the variance-reduction result, hence Rao-Blackwell.

questions.q: When the sufficient statistic used in Rao-Blackwellization is also complete, the result is strengthened by which theorem?
questions.explanation: With a complete sufficient statistic, the Lehmann-Scheffe theorem guarantees a unique minimum-variance unbiased estimator.

questions.q: The 'Rao' in the matrix concept 'Moore-Penrose / Rao' work relates to which area of linear algebra he advanced?
questions.explanation: Rao contributed extensively to the theory and applications of generalized (g-) inverses of matrices.

questions.q: In approximately which year was C. R. Rao born?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao was born in 1920 in present-day Karnataka, India.

questions.q: Rao's classification using a measure of 'diversity' and its decomposition is known as which entropy/diversity quantity?
questions.explanation: Rao's quadratic entropy is a diversity measure that generalizes several classical indices and allows decomposition.

questions.q: The Cramer-Rao bound's role in physics-style estimation problems makes it foundational to which engineering discipline?
questions.explanation: The Cramer-Rao bound is a standard benchmark for estimator performance in signal processing.

questions.q: From which Indian university did C. R. Rao earn his master's degree in mathematics before studying statistics?
questions.explanation: Rao earned an MSc in mathematics from Andhra University, then an MA in statistics from the University of Calcutta before going to Cambridge.

questions.q: The Cramer-Rao inequality is most naturally derived using which fundamental inequality from analysis?
questions.explanation: The classical proof of the Cramer-Rao bound applies the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality to the score and the estimator.
